title: PAI: The Life OS for AI-Powered Development
summary: PAI (Personal AI Infrastructure) is a Life OS built on Claude Code that provides AI agents with persistent memory across sessions, custom skills, structured workflows, and a local Pulse dashboard. It implements a seven-phase algorithm (observe, think, plan, build, execute, verify, learn) to help developers avoid re-explaining their projects, architecture, and coding standards to AI agents.
writeup: This video covers PAI (Personal AI Infrastructure), a Life OS built on Claude Code that gives AI agents persistent memory across sessions, custom skills, structured workflows, and a local Pulse dashboard. The core idea is a seven-phase algorithm (observe, think, plan, build, execute, verify, learn) that structures how agents work. The aim is to stop developers from re-explaining their projects, architecture, and coding standards to AI agents on every session by storing that context persistently.
